name: Alaska Gratin
recipeIngredient: 450 g Old potatoes, peeled and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the canned salmon.
2. Mix the juice with milk and yogurt.
3. Melt the butter on HIGH POWER for 30 seconds. Stir in the mushrooms, courgettes, and chives, and cook for 2 minutes on HIGH POWER.
4. Flake in the salmon. Arrange one third of the potato slices into the base of a microwave-proof dish. Spread half of the salmon mixture over the potatoes. Top with the remaining potato slices. Add the rest of the salmon, cover with the rest of the potatoes, and pour over the salmon stock. Sprinkle with cheese.
5. Cover and cook on MEDIUM POWER for 15 minutes. Uncover and cook for an additional 5 minutes. Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
6. Serves 4. Approximately 340 kcals per serving.
Note: This recipe is from "On the Wild Side - Alaska Canned Salmon Recipes" and was reprinted with permission from the Alaska Seafood Marketing Institute. The Meal-Master compatible recipe format is courtesy of Karen Mintzias.
